comments.author: Product Growth
comments.topic: Two types of PMs / AI tooling wave
comments.comment: The copy-paste PM vs workflow-native PM split is sharp. Installing skills helps — but the gap compounds when one team routes MCP output into the decision doc and the other into Slack screenshots. What's the first workflow you wired end-to-end with agents?

comments.author: Jason Knight
comments.comment: Jason — fair pushback. Installing skills lowers the floor, not the ceiling. The compounding part is what you do with the output: same insight in a decision doc vs a screenshot in Slack still diverges over weeks. Do you think "late" is real if the workflow ships customer-facing changes faster?

comments.author: Guillermo Flor
comments.comment: Guillermo — treating Claude like a search bar is the symptom; the fix is context routing, not better prompts. I've seen PMs get real leverage when PRD snippets, user quotes, and decision criteria are in the thread before the first ask. What's the one artifact you'd never start a Claude session without?

comments.author: Ayush Muniya
comments.comment: @Ayush Muniya — the RBI tokenization shift is the kind of constraint PMs skip until compliance forces a rewrite. The interesting product lesson isn't what Swiggy stores — it's what you can still infer from payment metadata. Did merchants actually change UX after 2022?

comments.author: Ivan S.
comments.topic: Sam Altman / Dario Amodei AI job-loss narrative ahead of IPOs
comments.comment: @Ivan S. — the timing read is fair, but the underlying shift isn't hype vs doom — it's which workflows get automated first. I've seen teams treat "AI won't replace you" as permission to skip workflow design. Which job tasks in your world are actually safer because judgment is embedded in the process, not the title?

comments.author: Sweta Mohanty
comments.topic: Director of Product Management at Zepto
comments.comment: @Sweta Mohanty — congrats on Director PM at Zepto. The interesting product bet at 10-min delivery isn't speed alone — it's what gets deprioritized when inventory and routing become the core loop. What's the first problem you inherited that looks like a roadmap item but is actually an ops constraint?
